info.title: Chess Games Database API
info.description: Aggregation service for chess games from multiple sources

get.description:
Comprehensive health check endpoint.

Checks:
- Database connection and query
- Redis connection
- Celery workers status
- Service statistics

Get Redis cache statistics.

Returns:
    Cache statistics including key counts and memory usage

post.description:
Create a new player with optional account mappings.

Args:
    player_data: Player creation data including optional accounts list
    service: Player service dependency

Returns:
    Created player with account mappings

get.description:
Get player by FIDE ID with smart auto-discovery.

**Smart API Workflow:**
1. Check database for player
2. If not found and auto_create=True → Fetch from FIDE website and create
3. If newly created and auto_sync=True → Automatically sync games from all sources in background
4. Return player data immediately (games sync in background)

**Example:** You're playing against someone tomorrow. Just call GET /players/4406656
and the API will:
- Find them on FIDE
- Create the player
- Start syncing all their games from Lichess, Chess.com, Chess-results
- Return player info immediately

Args:
    fide_id: FIDE ID of the player
    service: Player service dependency
    auto_create: Whether to auto-create player from FIDE website if not found
    auto_sync: Whether to automatically sync games when player is created
    
Returns:
    Player data (games sync in background if newly created)
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: If player not found and auto_create=False or FIDE lookup fails

parameters.name: auto_create

schema.description: Auto-create player from FIDE if not found
schema.title: Auto Create

parameters.description: Auto-create player from FIDE if not found

parameters.name: auto_sync

schema.description: Auto-sync games from all sources when player is created
schema.title: Auto Sync

parameters.description: Auto-sync games from all sources when player is created

Find player by Chess.com username.

Raises:

Search players by name (case-insensitive partial match).

Args:

Returns:

schema.title: Response Search Players Api V1 Players Search  Get

post.description:
Sync player games from a data source.
    
    This endpoint initiates a sync job that fetches games from the specified
```
    source (lichess, chesscom, or chess-results) and stores them in the database.
    
    Args:
        fide_id: FIDE ID of the player
        sync_request: Sync request data
        service: Player service dependency
        
    Returns:
        Sync job information
        
    Raises:
        HTTPException: If player not found or invalid source

get.description:
Get paginated list of games for a player with optional filters.

Args:
    fide_id: Player's FIDE ID
    page: Page number (starting from 1)
    page_size: Number of games per page (max 200)
    source: Filter by source (lichess, chesscom, chess-results)
    result: Filter by result
    date_from: Filter games from this date
    date_to: Filter games up to this date
    color: Filter by player color
    time_control: Filter by time control
    event: Filter by event name (partial match)
    service: Game service dependency
    
Returns:
    Paginated list of games

Get a single game by ID including PGN.

Args:
    game_id: Game ID
    service: Game service dependency
    
Returns:
    Game data with PGN
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: If game not found

Get PGN for a specific game as plain text.

Args:
    game_id: Game ID
    service: Game service dependency
    
Returns:
    PGN as plain text
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: If game not found

Download all games for a player as a single PGN file with streaming.

**Performance Features:**
- Streams games incrementally (starts sending data immediately)
- Constant memory usage regardless of game count
- Optional caching for repeated downloads (15min TTL)
- Optimized for 1000s of games per player

Args:
    fide_id: Player's FIDE ID
    source: Filter by source
    date_from: Filter games from this date
    date_to: Filter games up to this date
    use_cache: Use Redis cache if available (default: True)
    service: Game service dependency
    
Returns:
    Streaming PGN file with all games (one PGN per game, separated by blank lines)

Get sync job status and statistics.

Args:
    job_id: Job UUID
    service: Player service dependency
    
Returns:
    Sync job data
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: If job not found

post.description:
Import PGN file from URL (S3, R2, HTTP, sync.com).

**Supported Downloads:**
- Direct URLs: S3, R2, HTTP/HTTPS
- Sync.com: Provide full URL + cookies parameter

**Sync.com Example:**
```python
{
    "url": "https://m224.sync.com/u/FILE.pgn?cachekey=...&datakey=...",
    "source": "omotb",
    "cookies": "sync_auth=...; signature=..."
}
```

Use `scripts/test_har_parser.py` to extract URL and cookies from HAR file.

Downloads file in chunks to Railway temp storage,
then processes with existing PGN import service.
Runs as background Celery task to survive disconnections.

Args:
    request: PGN import request with URL and source

Returns:
    Job ID for tracking progress

get.description:
Get status of PGN import job.

Args:
    job_id: Celery task ID

Returns:
    Job status, progress, and results

post.description:
Cancel running PGN import job.

Args:
    job_id: Celery task ID

Returns:
    Cancellation confirmation

Link games to FIDE players using bulk SQL operations.

This endpoint triggers a Celery task that processes games in batches
and matches them to FIDE player records based on player names.

Args:
    request: Player linking request with source and batch size

Returns:
    Job ID for tracking progress

Example:
    ```
    POST /api/v1/admin/link-players
    {
        "source": "omotb",
        "batch_size": 100000
    }
    ```

Import Lichess broadcast archive for a specific month.

This endpoint downloads and imports official Lichess broadcast games
(tournaments, championships, etc.) for the specified year/month.

Features:
- Uses persistent volume (/app/data) - won't re-download on restart
- Extracts FIDE IDs from PGN headers for automatic player linking
- Deduplicates games automatically
- Runs as background Celery task

Args:
    request: Import request with year, month, and batch size

Returns:
    Job ID for tracking progress

Example:
    ```
    POST /api/v1/admin/import-lichess-broadcast
    {
        "year": 2025,
        "month": 1,
        "batch_size": 1000
    }
    ```

Trigger an import that downloads and imports all Lichess broadcast archives
in the given year range in a single consolidated task.

Import or update FIDE players database.

This endpoint downloads the latest FIDE players list (XML format)
and imports/updates all player records in the database.

Features:
- Downloads latest FIDE data (~1 million players)
- Uses PostgreSQL UPSERT for efficient updates
- Processes in batches to avoid memory issues
- Runs as background Celery task

Args:
    request: Import request with skip_download option
    
Returns:
    Job ID for tracking progress
    
Example:
    ```
    POST /api/v1/admin/import-fide-players
    {
        "skip_download": false
    }
    ```

get.description: Given a FEN position, return move statistics from the master games database (games with both players ≥ 2300 Elo, year ≥ 2000). Uses pre-aggregated data — sub-millisecond DB query.

schema.description: FEN string of the position to query

post.description: Start a background Celery task that retroactively indexes all qualifying historical games (both players ≥ min_rating, year ≥ since_year) into the explorer_positions table. Safe to call multiple times — already-indexed games are skipped.

parameters.name: since_year

schema.description: Index games from this year onwards
schema.title: Since Year

parameters.description: Index games from this year onwards

parameters.name: start_after_id

schema.description: Resume from this game ID (0 = start from beginning)
schema.title: Start After Id

parameters.description: Resume from this game ID (0 = start from beginning)

post.description: Back-fill the game_position_index table for games that were already indexed into explorer_positions before this table existed. The regular backfill skips already-indexed games, so this separate task is needed for existing installations. Safe to run multiple times — already-processed games are skipped via ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Poll progress via GET /api/v1/explorer/backfill/status/{task_id}.

parameters.name: min_rating

schema.description: Minimum rating for both players (0 = no filter)
schema.title: Min Rating

parameters.description: Minimum rating for both players (0 = no filter)

parameters.name: since_year

schema.description: Process games from this year onwards
schema.title: Since Year

parameters.description: Process games from this year onwards

parameters.name: start_after_id

schema.description: Resume from this game ID (0 = start from beginning)
schema.title: Start After Id

parameters.description: Resume from this game ID (0 = start from beginning)
